channel.title: INtopBackup
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtopBackup, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Pentagon shifts toward maintaining ties to Scouting
item.description: Months after NPR reported on the Pentagon's efforts to sever ties with Scouting America, efforts to maintain the partnership have new momentum

item.title: Every business wants your review. What's with the feedback frenzy?
item.description: Customers want to read reviews and businesses need reviews to attract customers. But the constant demand for reviews could be creating a feedback back...

item.title: Why farmers in California are backing a giant solar farm
item.description: Many farmers have had to fallow land as a state law comes into effect limiting their access to water. There's now a push to develop some of that land ...

item.title: U.S. and Iran to hold nuclear talks. And, Harvard professor resigns over Epstein ties
item.description: U.S. and Iranian officials are set to meet today in Geneva to discuss Tehran's nuclear program. And, Harvard professor Larry Summers is resigning over...

item.title: Tariffs cost American shoppers. They're unlikely to get that money back
item.description: After the Supreme Court declared the emergency tariffs illegal, the refund process will be messy and will go to businesses first.

item.title: What is the price of silver today: February 26, 2026?
item.description: The price of silver, following a drop from a record high, is on the rise again. Here's where it sits as of February 26.

item.title: Memorial services for Jesse Jackson begin at Chicago headquarters of his civil rights organization
Four convicted over spyware scandal that shook Greece
item.description: In what became known as "Greece's Watergate", surveillance software called Predator was used to target 87 people.

item.title: World Economic Forum boss quits after review of Epstein links
item.description: Brende acknowledged communications with Epstein but said he was "completely unaware" of his past criminal activity.

item.title: Kilmar Abrego Garcia back in court in bid to have criminal case dropped
item.description: A federal judge is weighing whether to dismiss the criminal case against Kilmar Abrego Garcia on the grounds the prosecution is vindictive.

item.title: Hillary Clinton appears before US House panel investigating Epstein
item.description: The former secretary of state recently dropped her longstanding refusal to appear, alongside her former president husband.

item.title: Russia and Ukraine exchange more than 1,000 soldiers' bodies
item.description: Russia's announcement comes as Ukraine's top negotiator met US peace envoys in Geneva.

item.title: Columbia student detained by DHS agents who made "misrepresentations," university president says
item.description: Columbia University's president says a student was detained by federal agents Thursday morning at one of its residential buildings.

item.title: Ford recalls 4.4 million vehicles over faulty software
item.description: Ford said a tech glitch can increase the risk of a crash for vehicles towing a trailer. Here's what to look for.

item.title: Most Americans see Iran as an enemy but doubt Trump’s judgment on military force, AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: A new AP-NORC poll finds that as the U.S. and Iran head into their next round of nuclear talks in Geneva, many U.S. adults continue to view Iran’s nuc...

item.title: Cuba says group shot on US-registered speedboat planned 'armed infiltration'
item.description: The passengers were Cuban nationals living in the US, the Cuban government says.

item.title: Trump pitches $1,000 match for some retirement plans
item.description: President Trump's State of the Union address included mentions of a $1,000 match for some retirement plans and more on the administration's economic p...

item.title: What to know about the LA superintendent whose home was searched by FBI
